# BL-088 — ecc update Feature Diagrams

## 1. Update Flow Sequence Diagram

This diagram illustrates the complete update flow from CLI invocation through rollback handling:

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant CLI as ecc CLI
    participant Orchestrator
    participant FileLock as FileLock
    participant Client as GithubReleaseClient
    participant Extractor as TarballExtractor
    participant Swap as Swap Module
    participant Installer as ecc install
    participant Rollback as Rollback

    CLI->>Orchestrator: execute_update(version)
    Orchestrator->>FileLock: acquire_lock()
    FileLock-->>Orchestrator: Ok(lock_guard)

    Orchestrator->>Orchestrator: check_write_permission()

    Orchestrator->>Client: latest_version() or get_version(v)
    Client-->>Orchestrator: ReleaseInfo

    Orchestrator->>Client: download_tarball(url)
    Client-->>Orchestrator: (streamed to disk)

    Orchestrator->>Client: verify_checksum(tarball, checksums_file)
    Client-->>Orchestrator: Ok(valid)

    Orchestrator->>Client: verify_cosign(tarball, bundle)
    Client-->>Orchestrator: Ok(verified) or SecurityError

    Orchestrator->>Extractor: extract_tarball(tarball, temp_dir)
    Extractor-->>Orchestrator: Ok(bin/ecc, bin/ecc-workflow)

    Orchestrator->>Swap: perform_swap(install_dir, extracted_bins)
    Swap-->>Orchestrator: Ok(swapped_list) with .bak backups

    Orchestrator->>Installer: ecc install (config sync)
    alt ConfigSyncFailed
        Installer-->>Orchestrator: Err(ConfigSyncFailed)
        Orchestrator->>Rollback: rollback_swapped(install_dir, swapped_list)
        Rollback-->>Orchestrator: Ok(restored) or RollbackFailed
        Orchestrator-->>CLI: Err(rolled back to previous version)
    else ConfigSyncSuccess
        Installer-->>Orchestrator: Ok()
        Orchestrator->>Orchestrator: cleanup_backups(.bak files)
        Orchestrator-->>CLI: Ok(updated to new version)
    end

    FileLock->>FileLock: release_lock() on exit
```

## 2. Component Dependency Diagram

This diagram shows the hexagonal architecture layers for the update feature:

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph Domain["Domain Layer (ecc-domain)"]
        PlatformEnum["Platform + Architecture<br/>enums"]
        ArtifactName["ArtifactName<br/>enum + resolve()"]
        UpdateError["UpdateError<br/>variants"]
        PlatformEnum --> ArtifactName
        PlatformEnum --> UpdateError
    end

    subgraph Ports["Port Layer (ecc-ports)"]
        ReleasePort["ReleaseClient<br/>trait"]
        ExtractorPort["TarballExtractor<br/>trait"]
        EnvPort["Environment<br/>trait"]
        FileLockPort["FileLock<br/>trait"]
        ReleasePort -.re-export.-> PlatformEnum
        EnvPort -.re-export.-> PlatformEnum
    end

    subgraph App["App Layer (ecc-app)"]
        Orchestrator["UpdateOrchestrator<br/>execute_update()"]
        Extract["extract_tarball()<br/>app module"]
        Swap["perform_swap()<br/>+ rollback_swapped()"]
        Orchestrator --> Extract
        Orchestrator --> Swap
        Orchestrator --> ReleasePort
        Orchestrator --> ExtractorPort
        Orchestrator --> FileLockPort
        Orchestrator --> EnvPort
    end

    subgraph Infra["Infrastructure Layer (ecc-infra)"]
        GitHub["GithubReleaseClient<br/>ureq-based HTTP"]
        Flate["FlateExtractor<br/>flate2 + tar"]
        OsEnv["OsEnvironment<br/>std::env impl"]
        Flock["FlockLock<br/>std::fs flock"]
        GitHub -.implements.-> ReleasePort
        Flate -.implements.-> ExtractorPort
        OsEnv -.implements.-> EnvPort
        Flock -.implements.-> FileLockPort
    end

    subgraph CLI["CLI Layer (ecc-cli)"]
        UpdateCmd["update command<br/>wiring"]
        UpdateCmd --> Orchestrator
        UpdateCmd --> GitHub
        UpdateCmd --> Flate
        UpdateCmd --> Flock
    end

    Domain --> Ports
    Ports --> App
    App --> Infra
    Infra --> CLI
```

## 3. Update State Transitions

This diagram shows the key state transitions during the update process:

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> CheckPermissions

    CheckPermissions --> PermissionDenied: not writable
    PermissionDenied --> [*]: error

    CheckPermissions --> AcquireLock
    AcquireLock --> UpdateLocked: lock held
    UpdateLocked --> [*]: error

    AcquireLock --> QueryRelease
    QueryRelease --> DownloadTarball
    DownloadTarball --> VerifyChecksum
    VerifyChecksum --> ChecksumFailed: mismatch
    ChecksumFailed --> [*]: error

    VerifyChecksum --> VerifyCosign
    VerifyCosign --> SecurityVerificationFailed: invalid signature
    SecurityVerificationFailed --> [*]: error

    VerifyCosign --> ExtractTarball
    ExtractTarball --> ZipSlipDetected: path traversal
    ZipSlipDetected --> [*]: error

    ExtractTarball --> PerformSwap
    PerformSwap --> SwapFailed: corrupt/missing files
    SwapFailed --> [*]: error

    PerformSwap --> ConfigSync: binaries swapped
    ConfigSync --> RollbackRequired: ecc install fails
    RollbackRequired --> Rollback
    Rollback --> RollbackSuccess: restored from .bak
    RollbackSuccess --> [*]: error with rollback info

    Rollback --> RollbackFailed: .bak missing
    RollbackFailed --> [*]: error (both failures)

    ConfigSync --> CleanupBackups: ecc install succeeds
    CleanupBackups --> [*]: success
```

## 4. Cross-Crate Dependency Surface

This diagram shows which crates interact for the update feature:

```mermaid
graph LR
    CLI["ecc-cli<br/>(update command)"]
    App["ecc-app<br/>(orchestrator + extract)"]
    Domain["ecc-domain<br/>(Platform, ArtifactName)"]
    Ports["ecc-ports<br/>(ReleaseClient, TarballExtractor)"]
    Infra["ecc-infra<br/>(GithubReleaseClient, FlateExtractor)"]
    TestSupport["ecc-test-support<br/>(mocks)"]

    CLI --> App
    CLI --> Infra
    App --> Domain
    App --> Ports
    Infra --> Ports
    Infra --> Domain
    TestSupport --> Domain
    TestSupport --> Ports
    TestSupport --> App
```
